---
title: "DVC (Data Version Control)"
---

We use [DVC](https://dvc.org/) to version-control large data files (models, input datasets) without storing them in Git.
DVC tracks file hashes in `.dvc` files committed to Git, while the actual data lives on remote storage.

## Authentication

Create a file `.dvc/config.local` (already gitignored) with credentials for the remotes:

```ini
['remote "goodcloud"']
    user = nhi_api
    password = <RIBASIM_NL_CLOUD_PASS>
['remote "modeldata"']
    user = nhi_api
    password = <RIBASIM_NL_CLOUD_PASS>
['remote "minio"']
    region = eu-west-1
    endpointurl = https://s3.deltares.nl
    access_key_id = <MINIO_ACCESS_KEY>
    secret_access_key = <MINIO_SECRET_KEY>
```

The password is the same as `RIBASIM_NL_CLOUD_PASS` in your `.env` file.
The MinIO keys correspond to `MINIO_ACCESS_KEY` and `MINIO_SECRET_KEY` in `.env`.

::: {.callout-note}
Since `pixi run install` runs `dvc pull`, this configuration is needed **before** that step.
:::

## Pulling data

After authentication is set up, pull all tracked data:

```sh
pixi run dvc pull
```

If you have local changes that you want to overwrite, add `--force`.

## Remotes

Three remotes are configured in `.dvc/config`:

| Remote | URL | Purpose |
|:-------|:----|:--------|
| `minio` (default) | `s3://ribasim-nl/dvc` | Primary DVC cache on MinIO |
| `goodcloud` | The Good Cloud `/dvc` | Legacy DVC storage |
| `modeldata` | The Good Cloud `/Ribasim modeldata` | Source data not under DVC control |

## Pipeline

The DVC pipeline is defined in [`dvc.yaml`](https://github.com/Deltares/Ribasim-NL/blob/main/dvc.yaml). Stages run sequentially with dependencies:

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-dvc-pipeline
%%| fig-cap: "DVC pipeline DAG (click to zoom)"
%%| file: _dvc-dag.mmd
```

```{=html}
<style>
#fig-dvc-pipeline { cursor: zoom-in; }
#fig-dvc-pipeline.fullscreen {
  position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 9999;
  background: white; cursor: zoom-out;
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  padding: 2rem;
}
#fig-dvc-pipeline.fullscreen svg { max-width: 100%; max-height: 100%; }
</style>
<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => {
  const fig = document.getElementById("fig-dvc-pipeline");
  if (fig) fig.addEventListener("click", () => fig.classList.toggle("fullscreen"));
});
</script>
```

Reproduce the full pipeline:

```sh
pixi run dvc repro
```

Reproduce a single stage:

```sh
pixi run dvc repro dynamic@aa_en_maas
```

## Importing data from remote storage

Use `dvc import-url` to download a file from a remote URL and take it under DVC control.
The `remote://modeldata` alias refers to The Good Cloud storage that holds source data:

```sh
pixi run dvc import-url -f remote://modeldata/Zuiderzeeland/modellen/Zuiderzeeland_parameterized_2025_9_0 data/Zuiderzeeland/modellen/
```

## Pushing changes

After producing new outputs (running pipeline stages or adding new data), push to the remote:

```sh
pixi run dvc push
```

Do this before pushing the updated `dvc.lock` file to git, so the data hashes referenced therein are avaiable for everyone.
